is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of uncomplicated falciparum malaria; SNPs detected in dhfr and dhps of P. falciparum.

reports_target_markers.evidence: dhfr codons 108, 51, 59 (triple mutant) and dhps codons 437, 540 (double mutant) — all curated target positions (Pfdhfr:51,59,108; Pfdhps:437,540).

sub_country_location.evidence: Frequencies in Table 1 are reported by study year for Mpumalanga Province overall. Two clinics (Naas and Mangweni) are named in acknowledgments, but marker frequencies are not broken down by clinic — only pooled per year for the province.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: Mutation frequencies reported separately by study year 1998, 2000, and 2002 (Table 1), each a single collection season.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from patients recruited at clinics in Mpumalanga Province, South Africa (Naas and Mangweni Clinics).

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary in vivo therapeutic efficacy studies conducted in 1998, 2000, 2002.

duplicate_risk.evidence: Study 'nested' within the South East African Combination Antimalarial Therapy evaluation (grant A00024); prior efficacy studies from same programme cited (Mabuza et al. 2001, 2005; Govere et al. 1999) may draw on overlapping collections.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary tables referenced for finer spatial breakdown; the paper reports at province level only, with no indication of site-level frequency tables in a supplement.

assessment.summary: Primary SP efficacy study in Mpumalanga Province, South Africa (1998, 2000, 2002) reporting P. falciparum dhfr (51,59,108) and dhps (437,540) mutation frequencies per year with extractable numerator/denominator counts. However, frequencies are pooled at province level; samples came from two named clinics (Naas and Mangweni) but marker frequencies are not resolved below the provincial level.
exclusion_reasons: Sub-country resolution not met: mutation frequencies reported only for Mpumalanga Province as a whole per study year, not broken down to the individual clinic (Naas/Mangweni) or finer level.
